description_long: This is a modern civilian helicopter with a streamlined fuselage painted primarily in red with white and gray accents. The helicopter features a large cockpit with multiple dark-tinted windows, providing wide visibility for the pilot and passengers. Its main rotor consists of four long, slender blades, and the tail section includes a fenestron-style enclosed tail rotor for enhanced safety and noise reduction. The landing gear comprises two sturdy, black skids attached to the underside of the fuselage with crossbars for stability. The tail boom is elongated and tapers to a vertical stabilizer, which is also red and features a white circular pattern around the tail rotor. No visible registration numbers or alphanumeric markings are present on the exterior.
description: A red and white civilian helicopter with a sleek body, large tinted cockpit windows, four-blade main rotor, enclosed tail rotor, and black landing skids.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the helicopter, cockpit windows, main and tail rotors, and landing skids.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the tail boom, fenestron-style tail rotor, and the symmetry of the skids.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other profile of the helicopter and rotor arrangement.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the cockpit windows, nose shape, and the alignment of the main rotor blades.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow, straight main rotor blades, likely made of composite material or metal, colored dark gray or black, with no visible markings.
attribute_tail_rotor: Fenestron-style enclosed tail rotor with multiple short blades arranged in a circular pattern, white in color, protected within the tail fin structure.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, black landing skids made of metal or composite, connected by crossbars for stability, with simple, straight design and no visible footpads.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Five cockpit windows: two large side windows, two smaller front windows, and a central nose window, all dark-tinted with black framing and no visible markings.
